title_ar: تحفة المحتاج لابن حجر الهيتمي — الجزء 4
content_en:
Tuhfat al-Muhtaj achieved immediate and enduring recognition as the supreme reference of the Hijazi branch of the Shafi'i school. Ibn Hajar's long residence in Mecca, combined with the centrality of that city in the Islamic world, meant that his legal positions spread across a vast geographic range. Scholars from Southeast Asia, East Africa, and South Asia who came to study in Mecca returned home with the Tuhfa as their primary legal reference, and in these regions it became the definitive source for Shafi'i legal determinations.

The work generated a significant secondary literature. The most important of its super-commentaries are the hashiyas of Ibn Qasim al-Abbadi and Ash-Shirwani, which were produced in the generation following Ibn Hajar and which became standard companions to the Tuhfa in madrasa education. These hashiyas added notes, resolved textual difficulties, and extended Ibn Hajar's analysis. The standard printed edition of the Tuhfa includes these hashiyas printed in the margins, so that students reading the text have immediate access to the supplementary commentary.

The comparison with Nihayat al-Muhtaj by ar-Ramli became a productive genre in its own right. Scholars wrote works listing the points of difference between Ibn Hajar and ar-Ramli, helping students navigate the competing authorities. This genre reflects the maturity of the Shafi'i tradition: a school confident enough in its foundations to acknowledge internal diversity and to treat that diversity as a source of intellectual richness rather than a problem requiring resolution.

In contemporary Islamic scholarship, the Tuhfa remains a primary reference for Shafi'i fatwa and for advanced legal education in the Hijaz, Yemen, Indonesia, Malaysia, and East Africa. It is consulted in court proceedings, in formal fatwa institutions, and in personal religious guidance. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's mark on the Shafi'i tradition is second only to that of an-Nawawi himself. For students and scholars operating in contexts where the Hijazi or Southeast Asian Shafi'i tradition prevails, the Tuhfa is not merely a historical reference but a living authority: its positions continue to be cited in fatwa institutions, taught in traditional madrasas, and consulted by scholars seeking the most authoritative classical statement of Shafi'i law on any given question. This ongoing practical relevance, six centuries after Ibn Hajar's death, testifies to the extraordinary durability of his legal scholarship.
content_id:
Tuhfat al-Muhtaj mendapat pengakuan langsung dan abadi sebagai referensi tertinggi dari cabang Hijazi mazhab Syafi'i. Tinggalnya Ibn Hajar yang lama di Mekah, dikombinasikan dengan sentralitas kota tersebut dalam dunia Islam, berarti posisi-posisi hukumnya menyebar di rentang geografis yang sangat luas. Ulama-ulama dari Asia Tenggara, Afrika Timur, dan Asia Selatan yang datang untuk belajar di Mekah pulang dengan Tuhfah sebagai referensi hukum utama mereka, dan di kawasan-kawasan ini ia menjadi sumber definitif untuk penentuan-penentuan hukum Syafi'i.

Karya ini menghasilkan literatur sekunder yang signifikan. Yang paling penting dari super-komentarnya adalah hawasyi dari Ibn Qasim al-'Abbadi dan asy-Syirwani, yang diproduksi dalam generasi setelah Ibn Hajar dan yang menjadi pendamping standar Tuhfah dalam pendidikan madrasah. Hawasyi-hawasyi ini menambahkan catatan, menyelesaikan kesulitan tekstual, dan memperluas analisis Ibn Hajar. Edisi cetak standar Tuhfah menyertakan hawasyi-hawasyi ini yang dicetak di pinggir halaman, sehingga mahasiswa yang membaca teks memiliki akses langsung terhadap komentar tambahan.

Perbandingan dengan Nihayat al-Muhtaj karya ar-Ramli menjadi genre yang produktif tersendiri. Ulama-ulama menulis karya-karya yang mendaftar poin-poin perbedaan antara Ibn Hajar dan ar-Ramli, membantu mahasiswa menavigasi otoritas-otoritas yang bersaing. Genre ini mencerminkan kematangan tradisi Syafi'i: sebuah mazhab yang cukup percaya diri pada fondasinya untuk mengakui keragaman internal dan memperlakukan keragaman tersebut sebagai sumber kekayaan intelektual daripada masalah yang memerlukan penyelesaian.

Dalam keilmuan Islam kontemporer, Tuhfah tetap menjadi referensi utama untuk fatwa Syafi'i dan untuk pendidikan hukum tingkat lanjut di Hijaz, Yaman, Indonesia, Malaysia, dan Afrika Timur. Ia dikonsultasikan dalam persidangan pengadilan, dalam institusi-institusi fatwa formal, dan dalam bimbingan agama pribadi. Jejak Ibn Hajar al-Haytami pada tradisi Syafi'i hanya kalah dari an-Nawawi sendiri. Bagi mahasiswa dan ulama yang beroperasi dalam konteks di mana tradisi Syafi'i Hijazi atau Asia Tenggara berlaku, Tuhfah bukan sekadar referensi historis tetapi otoritas yang hidup: posisi-posisinya terus dikutip dalam institusi-institusi fatwa, diajarkan di madrasah-madrasah tradisional, dan dikonsultasikan oleh ulama-ulama yang mencari pernyataan klasik paling otoritatif tentang hukum Syafi'i untuk pertanyaan apa pun. Relevansi praktis yang berkelanjutan ini, enam abad setelah kematian Ibn Hajar, bersaksi tentang ketahanan luar biasa keilmuan hukumnya.
content_ar: Tuhfat al-Muhtaj achieved immediate and enduring recognition as the supreme reference of the Hijazi branch of the الشافعي school. Ibn Hajar's long residence in مكة المكرمة, combined with the centrality of that city in the Islamic الدنيا, meant that his legal positions spread across a vast geographic range. العلماء from Southeast Asia, East Africa, and South Asia who came to study in مكة المكرمة returned home with the Tuhfa as their الأساسي legal reference, and in these regions it became the definitive source for الشافعي legal determinations. The work generated a المهم secondary literature. The most المهم of its super-commentaries are the hashiyas of Ibn Qasim al-Abbadi and Ash-Shirwani, which were produced in the generation following Ibn Hajar and which became standard الصحابة to the Tuhfa in madrasa education. These hashiyas added notes, resolved textual difficulties, and extended Ibn Hajar's analysis. The standard printed edition of the Tuhfa includes these hashiyas printed in the margins, لذا that students reading the text have immediate access to the supplementary commentary. The comparison with Nihayat al-Muhtaj by ar-Ramli became a productive genre in its own الحق. العلماء wrote works listing the points of difference between Ibn Hajar and ar-Ramli, helping students navigate the competing authorities. This genre reflects the maturity of the الشافعي التراث: a school confident enough in its foundations to acknowledge internal diversity and to treat that diversity as a source of intellectual richness rather than a problem requiring resolution. In contemporary Islamic scholarship, the Tuhfa remains a الأساسي reference for الشافعي الفتوى and for advanced legal education in the Hijaz, Yemen, Indonesia, Malaysia, and East Africa. It is consulted in court proceedings, in formal الفتوى institutions, and in personal religious الهداية. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's mark on the الشافعي التراث is ثانياً only to that of النووي himself. For students and العلماء operating in contexts where the Hijazi or Southeast Asian الشافعي التراث prevails, the Tuhfa is not merely a historical reference but a living authority: its positions continue to be cited in الفتوى institutions, taught in التقليدي madrasas, and consulted by العلماء seeking the most المعتمد الكلاسيكي statement of الشافعي law on any given question. This ongoing practical relevance, six centuries بعد Ibn Hajar's الموت, testifies to the extraordinary durability of his legal scholarship.
